---
title: "Nedim Mouse Lungs Correlation Analysis"
output: html_notebook
---

# Packages 
```{r}
#library("BiocManager")
rm(list = ls(all.names = TRUE))
gc()
#.libPaths("/home/mbotos/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/"4.2"/")
```



```{r}
.libPaths(,c("C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Documents/R/4.4.0/"))
```

```{r}
# BiocManager::install(c("dorothea"),force=TRUE,ask = TRUE)
# library("dorothea")
library("BiocManager")
library("dplyr")
library("tidyr")
#unloadNamespace("edgeR")
#unloadNamespace("limma")
#library("edgeR")
library("ggplot2")
library("ggrepel")
library("svglite")
library("biomaRt")
library("corrplot")
#install.packages("ggpubr")
library("ggpubr")
library("gridExtra")
```

## Read excel file
```{r}
sample_sheet = "C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Nedim/2026/data/All lungs and brain_cleaned_Dec2025_NK.xlsx"
sample_sheet_lungs = readxl::read_excel(sample_sheet, col_names = TRUE, sheet = 2)
head(sample_sheet_lungs)
colnames(sample_sheet_lungs)
table(sample_sheet_lungs$`ID IVI`,sample_sheet_lungs$`Euthanasia day (dpi)`)


```

## Make groups for animals
### Early (0-4)
### Resolution (5-7)
### Clearanace (8-max)

```{r}
table(sample_sheet_lungs$`Vaccinated (v), none (n)`)
# sample_sheet_lungs$Euthanasia_day_dpi = gsub(x = sample_sheet_lungs$`Euthanasia day (dpi)`,
#                                              pattern ="?p\\.v$",
#                                              replacement = "")
sample_sheet_lungs$Euthanasia_day_dpi <- as.numeric(sample_sheet_lungs$`Euthanasia day (dpi)`)
mterm_val <- max(sample_sheet_lungs$Euthanasia_day_dpi)

#sample_sheet_lungs$`Vaccinated (v), none (n)`

message("Add n=12")

sample_sheet_lungs <- sample_sheet_lungs  |> dplyr::mutate(
    # Time group based on euthanasia day
    Time_Group = case_when(
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 0 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= 4 ~ "Early infection (0-4 dpi)",
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 5 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= 7 ~ "Peak of disease (5-7 dpi)",
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 8 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= mterm_val ~ paste0("Resolution (8-",mterm_val," dpi)"),
      TRUE ~ "Other"),
    # Vaccination status
    Vaccination_Status = if_else(`Vaccinated (v), none (n)` == "n", "n", "v") |> factor())

# Create combined grouping variable
sample_sheet_lungs <- sample_sheet_lungs  |> dplyr::mutate(
    Combined_Group = paste(Time_Group, "-", Vaccination_Status)
  )

table(sample_sheet_lungs$Vaccination_Status)
```
```{r}
max(sample_sheet_lungs$`Manual Score (0 to 4)`)
```

```{r}
# Select key measures for correlation analysis
output_dir <- "C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Nedim/2026/Results/Lungs/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)){dir.create(output_dir,recursive = TRUE)}

colnames(sample_sheet_lungs)

Mergecorrelation_vars <- c(
  "Manual score (0 to 4)",
  "Histopathological score",
  "qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)",
  "qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)",
  "Live Virus (PFU/ml)",
  "% Positivity in total parenchyma"
)

#  "Histopathological score",
sample_sheet_lungs$`qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)` <- as.numeric(sample_sheet_lungs$`qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)`)
sample_sheet_lungs$`qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)` <- as.numeric(sample_sheet_lungs$`qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)`)
sample_sheet_lungs$`Live virus (PFU/ml)` <- as.numeric(sample_sheet_lungs$`Live virus (PFU/ml)`)

Mergecorrelation_vars <- colnames(sample_sheet_lungs)[c(3,14,15,16,17,11)]


# Function to create correlation matrix plot
create_correlation_plot <- function(data, group_name,correlation_vars=Mergecorrelation_vars) {
  # Select and clean data
  n_samples <- length(unique(data$'ID IVI'))

  cor_data <- data |> dplyr::select(all_of(correlation_vars)) #|> #tidyr::drop_na()
  
  if(nrow(cor_data) < 2) {
    message(paste("Not enough data for group:", group_name))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Calculate correlation matrix
  cor_matrix <- cor(cor_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")#pearson
  
  # Create plot
  pdf(paste0(output_dir,"correlation_matrix_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", group_name), ".pdf"),
      width = 12, height = 10)
  corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, 
           method = "color",
           type = "lower",  # Half triangle (lower)
           tl.col = "black",
           tl.srt = 45,
           tl.cex = 0.8,
           addCoef.col = "black",
           number.cex = 0.7,
           col = colorRampPalette(c("#6D9EC1", "white", "#E46726"))(200),
           title = paste("Correlation Matrix -", group_name,"n=",n_samples),
           mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0))
  
  dev.off()
  
  return(cor_matrix)
}

# Generate correlation matrices for each group
groups <- unique(sample_sheet_lungs$Combined_Group)

correlation_results <- list()
for(group in groups) {
  group_data <- sample_sheet_lungs |> dplyr::filter(Combined_Group == group)
  correlation_results[[group]] <- create_correlation_plot(data = group_data, group_name = group)
}
#length(unique(sample_sheet_lungs$`ID IVI`[sample_sheet_lungs$Combined_Group=="Accute (0-4 dpi) - n"]))

# Overall correlation (all data)
correlation_results[["Overall"]] <- create_correlation_plot(sample_sheet_lungs, "Overall")
#length(unique(sample_sheet_lungs$`ID IVI`))
#dim(sample_sheet_lungs)

# By vaccination status only
for(vac_status in c("n", "v")) {
  vac_data <- sample_sheet_lungs |> dplyr::filter(Vaccination_Status == vac_status)
  vac_label <- ifelse(vac_status == "v", "Vaccinated", "Non-vaccinated")
  correlation_results[[vac_label]] <- create_correlation_plot(vac_data, vac_label)
}

# By time group only
time_groups <- c("Early (0-4 dpi)", "Resolution (5-7 dpi)", "Clearance (8-15 dpi)")
for(time_group in time_groups) {
  time_data <- sample_sheet_lungs  |> dplyr::filter(Time_Group == time_group)
  correlation_results[[time_group]] <- create_correlation_plot(time_data, time_group)
}


```


# COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS: HISTOPATHOLOGICAL SCORES VS % POSITIVE AREA
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS: HISTOPATHOLOGICAL SCORES VS % POSITIVE AREA
# ============================================================================

# Prepare data for comparison
#sample_sheet_lungs$`Live virus (PFU/ml)`
#sample_sheet_lungs$`Manual Score (0 to 4)`
comparison_data <- sample_sheet_lungs |> dplyr::select(
  ID,
  `ID IVI`,
  `Manual Score (0 to 4)`,
  `Histopathological score`,
  `qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)`,
  `qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)`,
  `Live virus (PFU/ml)`,
  `% Positivity in total parenchyma`,
  Time_Group,
  Vaccination_Status)# |> drop_na(`% Positive area.y`)



# Rename for easier plotting
names(comparison_data) <- c(
  "ID",
  "ID_IVI",
  "manual semiquantification lung",
  "Histopathological score",
  "qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)",
  "qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)",
  "Live Virus (PFU/ml)",
  "digital quantification lung",
  "Time_Group",
  "Vaccination_Status"
)

# Calculate correlations for each measure
cor_manual_score <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$`manual semiquantification lung`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
cor_HPS <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$`Histopathological score`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
cor_qPCR_swabs <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$`qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
cor_qPCR_lung <-  cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$`qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
cor_live_virus <-  cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$`Live Virus (PFU/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")


# Calculate correlations for each measure
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai <- list()
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_manual_score_hps"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`manual semiquantification lung`, comparison_data$`Histopathological score`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_manual_score_qpcr"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`manual semiquantification lung`, comparison_data$`qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_manual_score_live_virus"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`manual semiquantification lung`,comparison_data$`Live Virus (PFU/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")

scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_ai_score_hps"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$`Histopathological score`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_ai_score_qpcr"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$`qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
scores_vs_manual_vs_ai[["cor_ai_score_live_virus"]] <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$`Live Virus (PFU/ml)`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")

cor_list <- list('manual semiquantification lung'=cor_manual_score,"Histopathological score"=cor_HPS,"qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)"=cor_qPCR_swabs,"qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)"=cor_qPCR_lung,"Live Virus (PFU/ml)"=cor_live_virus)
plot_list <- list()
# Create individual plots
for (plt in setdiff(names(cor_list),"ID_IVI")){
  
  plot_list[[paste0(plt)]] <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = `digital quantification lung`, y = .data[[plt]])) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Time_Group, shape = Vaccination_Status), size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = paste0(plt," vs Algorithm"),
         x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
         y = plt) + #      subtitle = sprintf("Spearman r = %.3f", cor_list[[plt]])) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)) +   # Pearson with "P:" prefix
    # Pearson with "P:" prefix
    ggpubr::stat_cor(
      method = "pearson",
      output.type = "text", # Required to use custom string prefixes
      label.x.npc = 0.1, 
      label.y.npc = 0.95,
      aes(label = paste0("P: ", after_stat(r.label), ", ", after_stat(p.label)))) +
    # Spearman with "S:" prefix
    ggpubr::stat_cor(
      method = "spearman",
      output.type = "text", # Required to use custom string prefixes
      label.x.npc = 0.1, 
      label.y.npc = 0.88,
      aes(label = paste0("S: ", after_stat(r.label), ", ", after_stat(p.label))))
}


# Combine all three plots into one figure
combined_plot <- ggarrange(
  plot_list[[1]], 
  plot_list[[2]],
  plot_list[[3]],
  plot_list[[4]],
  plot_list[[5]],
  #plot_brain_24,
  ncol = 5, 
  nrow = 1,
  common.legend = TRUE,
  legend = "bottom")

# Add main title
combined_plot <- annotate_figure(
  combined_plot,
  top = text_grob("Algorithm Performance vs Manual Scores", 
                  face = "bold", size = 12)
)

# Save the combined plot
ggsave(paste0(output_dir,"histopath_vs_algorithm_comparison.pdf"), 
       combined_plot, 
       width = 15, 
       height = 5, 
       dpi = 1200)

```
```{r}
head(comparison_data$Vaccination_Status)
```

# Plot v2 using decided features.
```{r}

# Create individual plots
#for (plt in names(scores_vs_manual_vs_ai)){
plot_list_v2 <- list()
comparison_data$qPCR_Lung
comparison_data$qPCR_swabs
comparison_data$Live_virus


plot_values <- c("Histopathological score", "qPCR swabs (RNA/ml)", "qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)", "Live Virus (PFU/ml)")
x_axes <- c('manual semiquantification lung', 'digital quantification lung')

for (j in x_axes) {
  for (plt in plot_values) {
    
    # Create a unique key for the list
    plot_key <- paste0(j, "_", plt)
    
    n_samples = length(unique(comparison_data[["ID"]][!is.na(comparison_data[[plt]]) & comparison_data[[plt]] != ""]))

    # Decide whether to log-transform 
    #y_var <- if (plt == "Histopath_Brain") { .data[[plt]] } else { log10(.data[[plt]]) }
    # Compute y outside ggplot, .data[[]] only exists in the ggplots
    #y_var <- if (plt %in% c("Histopath_Brain","qPCR_Lung")) { comparison_data[[plt]] } else { log10(comparison_data[[plt]]) } values already log.
    
    
    #plot_list_v2[[plot_key]] <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = .data[[j]], y = log10(.data[[plt]]))) +
    plot_list_v2[[plot_key]] <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = .data[[j]], y = .data[[plt]] )) +

      geom_jitter(aes(color = Time_Group, shape = Vaccination_Status), size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
      labs(title = paste0(plt, " vs ", gsub("_", " ", j)), # Dynamic title
           x = gsub("_", " ", j),                          # Dynamic X label
           y = if (plt == "Histopath_Brain") plt else paste0("log10 - ", plt), #qPCR_Lung is already log10ed
           subtitle = paste0("n=",n_samples)) +
      {if (j == "manual semiquantification lung") {xlim(0, 5)} else {NULL}} +
      facet_wrap(~ Vaccination_Status) +   #horizontal stack
      theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom",
            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)) +
      # Pearson with "P:" prefix
      ggpubr::stat_cor(method = "pearson",
                       output.type = "text", 
                       label.x.npc = "left", 
                       label.y.npc = 0.97,
                       aes(label = paste0("P: ", after_stat(r.label), ", ", after_stat(p.label)))) +
      
      # Spearman with "S:" prefix
      ggpubr::stat_cor(method = "spearman",
                       output.type = "text", 
                       label.x.npc = "left", 
                       label.y.npc = 0.87,
                       aes(label = paste0("S: ", after_stat(r.label), ", ", after_stat(p.label))))
}}

# Combine all six plots
# Note: plot_list_v2 is a named list; using names(plot_list_v2) ensures all are included
combined_plot <- ggarrange(
  plotlist = plot_list_v2, 
  ncol = 2, 
  nrow = 4,
  common.legend = TRUE,
  legend = "bottom"
)

# Add main title
combined_plot <- annotate_figure(
  combined_plot,
  top = text_grob("Lung Manual vs Digital Scores", 
                  face = "bold", size = 10))

# Save the combined plot - Increased height to 8 since you have 2 rows now
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "lung_histopath_vs_digital_comparison_v4_log10.pdf"), 
       combined_plot, 
       width = 9, 
       height = 16, 
       dpi = 600) # 1200 is very high; 600 is usually plenty for publication
message("In lung, values are already log 10 transformed for qPCR swab, qPCR Lung and Live virus")
```


## STATISTICS

```{r}
#qPCR swabs are a meassure that was taken in the animal besides the valoration was done on brain or lungs.
# Test if the qPCR_swabs got better results for the brain Manual score vs lung Manual score?
# Test if the qPCR_swabs got better results for the brain AI score vs lung AI score?


```


```{r}
# ============================================================================
# STATISTICS BY GROUP
# ============================================================================

# Calculate correlation statistics for each group
group_correlations <- comparison_data  |> 
  group_by(Time_Group, Vaccination_Status) |> 
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    cor_manual_score <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$`manual semiquantification lung`, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    cor_HPS <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`, comparison_data$HPS, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    cor_qPCR_swabs <- cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$qPCR_swabs, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    cor_qPCR_lung <-  cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$qPCR_Lung, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    cor_live_virus <-  cor(comparison_data$`digital quantification lung`,comparison_data$Live_virus, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"), # add any other
    .groups = "drop")

# Save results to CSV
write.csv(group_correlations, paste0(output_dir,"correlation_statistics_by_group.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
cat("\n=== CORRELATION SUMMARY ===\n")
cat(sprintf("\nOverall Correlations (Algorithm vs Manual Scores):\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Manual Score:      r = %.3f\n", cor_manual_score))
cat(sprintf("  HPS:      r = %.3f\n", cor_HPS))
cat(sprintf("  qPCR swabs:      r = %.3f\n", cor_qPCR_swabs))
cat(sprintf("  qPCR lung:      r = %.3f\n", cor_qPCR_lung))
cat(sprintf("  live virus:      r = %.3f\n", cor_live_virus))
cat("\n=== CORRELATIONS BY GROUP ===\n")
print(group_correlations)

```


```{r}
```


## VISUALIZATION by Group
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# ADDITIONAL VISUALIZATION: FACETED BY GROUP
# ============================================================================

# Create faceted plots

faceted_lung <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = `digital quantification lung`, y = `manual semiquantification lung` )) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +#, shape=sex
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Manual Score Lung vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
    x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
    y = "Manual Score Lung"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)

ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "manual_score_lung_faceted.pdf"), faceted_lung, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)
# 
# faceted_brain_23 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = `digital quantification lung`, y = Histopath_Brain_23)) +
#   geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status, shape=sex), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",linewidth = 0.5) +
#   facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
#   labs(
#     title = "Histopathological Score Brain (23) vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
#     x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
#     y = "Histopathological Score Brain (23)"
#   ) +
#   theme_bw() +
#   stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)
# 
# ggsave("histopath_brain_23_faceted.pdf", faceted_brain_23, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)

# faceted_brain_24 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = `digital quantification lung`, y = Histopath_Brain_24)) +
#   geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
#   facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
#   labs(
#     title = "Histopathological Score Brain (24) vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
#     x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
#     y = "Histopathological Score Brain (24)"
#   ) +
#   theme_bw() +
#   stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)

# ggsave(paste0(output_dir,"histopath_brain_24_faceted.pdf", faceted_brain_24, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)
```

```{r}
message("Code run correctly")
```


```{r}
print("Done")
```
```{r}

```
